findings.evidence: Lines 117-121 quantify only `(H)` and `x y : SubgroupFiberOrbitQuotient H b`, while lines 52-55 define `subgroupFiberOrbitMapToFiberOrbit` for every subgroup and lines 158-161 already compare forgotten classes from different `H K`.
findings.fix: State the criterion with two subgroups, e.g. `(H K : Subgroup (Deck p)) (x : SubgroupFiberOrbitQuotient H b) (y : SubgroupFiberOrbitQuotient K b)` and representatives `x = subgroupFiberOrbitClass H e`, `y = subgroupFiberOrbitClass K e'`; derive the same-`H` form if needed.
findings.issue: `subgroupFiberOrbitMapToFiberOrbit_eq_iff` only compares two classes from the same `H`, even though both forgetful maps land in the same `FiberOrbitQuotient p b` for arbitrary subgroups.

findings.evidence: Line 154 calls `hreg.fiberOrbitClass_eq`, whose proof in `FiberOrbit.lean:169-172` is just `Subsingleton.elim`; `IsRegular` is equivalent to `Function.Surjective p ∧ ∀ b, Subsingleton (FiberOrbitQuotient p b)` at `FiberOrbit.lean:155-159`.
findings.fix: Prove general versions under `[Subsingleton (FiberOrbitQuotient p b)]` for lines 149 and 158, then keep the `IsRegular` wrappers as corollaries via `hreg.subsingleton_fiberOrbitQuotient b` if desired.
findings.issue: The regular-collapse lemmas assume full `IsRegular p`, but the proof only uses that `FiberOrbitQuotient p b` is subsingleton; `IsRegular` also includes surjectivity and transitivity on every other fibre.

summary: The new API is mostly at the intended level, but two statements stop short of the natural generality exposed by their own codomain and nearby quotient API.
